title: Shinji Asuka is Adam Eve or Shinjis Psychedelic Sunbathe
display_title: Shinji Asuka is Adam Eve or Shinjis Psychedelic Sunbathe
wikitext:
[]: Shinji/Asuka is Adam/Eve, or, Shinji's Psychedelic Sunbathe             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Mon Jul 16, 2007 11:00 pm Okay, I was going to reply in an arguement between SSD and shinesman_cobalt about how Shinji and Asuka are/are not Adam and Eve. I initially was on SSD's side, because she's usually right about these things, but mulling it over, I'm siding with shinesman on this one. Original arguement can be found here [url]http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=267&start=160[/url] While Shinji and Asuka are not literally Adam and Eve, give some of these points a thought. -Shinji rejected Rei/Lilith during Instrumentality, and as a result was booted from the LCL Sea (Garden of Eden, Paradise, etc.) -Lilith rejected Adam after being created, and as a result was booted from the Garden of Eden (LCL Sea, Paradise, etc.) Its kind of a reverse vibe thing going on here. -Asuka wanted to be with Shinji (or something to that effect), and left Instrumentality (Garden of Eden, LCL Sea, etc) -Adam wanted to be with Eve (eating the fruit), and was booted from the Garden of Eden as a result, along with Eve. Another kind of reverse vibe coming out here. -Shinji and Asuka are the only two humans on Earth for the moment, and a new Earth has taken shape. -Adam and Eve are the only two humans on Earth for the moment, and a new Earth has taken shape. My next point kind of requires a little stretching, but it falls in here somewhere: -After Cain killed Abel, he was expelled into the wilderness, where he found a mate, and produced the Caananites. However, although the Bible explains this away as being a daughter of Adam and Eve, its a little sketchy, because it doesn't bring her up at anytime beforehand, or say why she is out in the wilderness (I could be wrong here, correct me if I'm wrong anybody) The Cain finding a mate part doesn't pertain to Shinji and Asuka, but the mysterious other person leads me to... -People coming back after Instrumentality. They could show up at any future time and influence the events in the lives of Shinji/Asuka (Adam/Eve) This leads me to conclude that Asuka and Shinji are new versions of Adam and Eve, or, if not, very close analogues. Admins/Mods, sorry if there is already a thread dedicated to this, I did search for it. If there is, link it, and I'll just copy and paste it to that thread. Your call. :?

[]: Re: Shinji/Asuka is Adam/Eve, or, Shinji's Psychedelic Sunba             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Jul 16, 2007 11:11 pm CorporalChaos wrote: -Shinji rejected Rei/Lilith during Instrumentality, and as a result was booted from the LCL Sea (Garden of Eden, Paradise, etc.) -Lilith rejected Adam after being created, and as a result was booted from the Garden of Eden (LCL Sea, Paradise, etc.) Its kind of a reverse vibe thing going on here.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Jul 18, 2007 12:46 pm It's always fun at this point to chuck in this little gem http://web.archive.org/web/20041206153315/http://www.eltonalteco.net/En/historiaev.php So Shinji and Asuka take bad, just as Adam and Lilith; and however Shinji and Rei were loved, although they never got it to express freely (Well, Rei says in "The end of Evangelion"). Then Shinji must kill and kick Asuka from the "paradise" so tha Rei can enter, who waits for the expulsion of Lilith.

[]:             Quote Post by sensei_juice » Wed Jul 18, 2007 1:25 pm Yui: "Anywhere can be paradise, as long as you're alive." Or something to that effect involving the word "paradise." I don't think that's a coincidence. I would agree that it's some sort of warped Eden - I wouldn't say it's the anti-Eden, though.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Jul 18, 2007 1:34 pm sensei_juice wrote: Yui: "Anywhere can be paradise, as long as you're alive." Or something to that effect involving the word "paradise." I don't think that's a coincidence.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jul 18, 2007 2:04 pm Its Earth. The only thing thats changed is there's that red ring of Lilith's LCL in the sky: http://evacommentary.org/episode-m26/episode-m26-scene11.html http://evacommentary.org/episode-m26/episode-m26-scene12.html

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Wed Jul 18, 2007 2:30 pm Tines wrote: If you're looking for metaphor here, this is anti-Eden at the End of the World.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jul 18, 2007 2:48 pm .....Keep in mind Tines means Anti-Eden as in "there's no hope AT ALL, everything sucks, Asuka's gonna die from childbirth in several years (This one is beyond me as who ever said those two were planning on having kids? There's such a thing known as Protection if they WOULD become sexually active...) , etc etc." while obviously there is some form of hope since: A) Yui made sure Shinji and the others would have a planet to actually live on. B) Asuka returned, obviously indicating others can (and WILL), too. (Must I bring out the Group Photo pic at each of these discussions? ) And what's all this about Anno giving the finger to fans? *eye twitch* Won't those rumors of extreme evilness just die?! The hatemail flashing on the screen in EoE was in terms of Death & Rebirth, NOT about EoTV. EoTV in fact, was actually praised with how it was done.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Jul 18, 2007 2:56 pm THE Hal E. Burton 9000 wrote: giving the proverbial finger to the fans for not "getting" the biggest point from EoTV).

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jul 18, 2007 3:10 pm There are sketched out animatics for a TV episode 25 that looks remarkably like the first half of EoE in 4:3 format --

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Jul 18, 2007 3:20 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: "Red dots"?

[]: My two cents             Quote Post by chee » Wed Jul 18, 2007 3:32 pm IMHO, a totally pessimistic ending does not work thematically at all. Although I will concede that the situation does SEEM grim, I doubt Anno had such an "easy" ending in mind. "LOL EVERYONE DIES @ TEH END!" seems way too simple. NGE is about despair AND hope, not just despair. Nowhere is it written that we see the state of the ENTIRE world. For all we know, we're just seeing the ruins of the Geofront/Tokyo 3 (which were pretty horribly messed up to begin with.) The fact that there is room for hope, no matter how naive or ill-founed, is the entire point of the ending. As for the A/S issue... I'm a "supporter" not because of any sentimental or fanboyish reasons, but because, again, it thematically/narratively makes sense. (Two similar caresses within 5 minutes of each other- intentional, I think.)

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Wed Jul 18, 2007 4:36 pm Stardust-chan wrote: And what's all this about Anno giving the finger to fans? *eye twitch* Won't those rumors of extreme evilness just die?! The hatemail flashing on the screen in EoE was in terms of Death & Rebirth, NOT about EoTV. EoTV in fact, was actually praised with how it was done.

[]: Re: Shinji/Asuka is Adam/Eve, or, Shinji's Psychedelic Sunba             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Wed Jul 18, 2007 4:56 pm Ornette wrote: I'm not sure what you mean by these 2. Rei asked if Shinji wanted to end Instrumentality, and he said OK. And the second point, I assume you're talking about the Judaic(?) Lilith and Adam?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Sep 16, 2007 11:35 pm i googled up somthing like this a long time ago from what i understood from it, when Shiji rejected Instrumentality, we basically ruined it for everyone, so everyone came back (except for those who had actaully died and not been a part of Instrumentality like Misato, Ritsuko and Gendo). its just happened to only be him and Asuka on the beach while everyone else was somewhere else.

[]:             Quote Post by Sandoz » Sun Sep 16, 2007 11:55 pm Sachiel_13 wrote: i googled up somthing like this a long time ago from what i understood from it, when Shiji rejected Instrumentality, we basically ruined it for everyone, so everyone came back (except for those who had actaully died and not been a part of Instrumentality like Misato, Ritsuko and Gendo). its just happened to only be him and Asuka on the beach while everyone else was somewhere else.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Sep 17, 2007 12:03 am i suppose thats true lets just say that if they did come back, they died again, because their bodies couldnt sustain life (Misato beign blown up, Ritsuko being shot, and Gendo only have legs)

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Mon Sep 17, 2007 2:59 am im fairly certain the answer to this question will be more clear after eva 4.0 provided instrumentality goes down the same way.

[]:             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Mon Sep 17, 2007 6:13 am Mr. Tines wrote: It's always fun at this point to chuck in this little gem http://web.archive.org/web/20041206153315/http://www.eltonalteco.net/En/historiaev.php

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Mon Sep 17, 2007 6:31 am Anno didn't say that, but someone from Gainax very clearly did. Nobody wants to listen...or maybe Anno was worried about more death threats.
